file_insights.changes_summary: Expanded from 34 to 734 lines; implemented complete ExtendedRenderer trait with 4 methods and 60+ unit tests
key_additions: state_color_16() function mapping states to 16-color ANSI codes (bright variants 90-97)
moon_glyph() function for phase progress visualization (◔◑◕●)
format_duration() for human-readable elapsed time formatting
render_section_header() with width-adaptive box drawing borders
render_card_row() with accent stripe, state markers, and metadata display
render_progress() with 16-cell progress bar and phase information
render_footer() with statistics summary and box drawing
60+ comprehensive unit tests covering ANSI codes, box chars, and content rendering
file_insights.test_coverage: Full coverage of happy paths, edge cases (zero counts, narrow/wide terminals, missing fields), and visual element presence assertions

patterns_discovered.name: State-based color and marker mapping
patterns_discovered.description: Each card state (pending, running, done, failed, merged) maps to a specific bright ANSI color (90-97) and visual marker (·▶✓✗)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Consistent visual language across renderer levels

patterns_discovered.name: Width-adaptive layout calculation
patterns_discovered.description: All box drawing uses width saturation arithmetic (w.saturating_sub()) to prevent panics on narrow terminals

patterns_discovered.name: Unicode glyph selection via conditional logic
patterns_discovered.description: Moon glyphs and progress bars use fractional thresholds (0.25, 0.5, 0.75) or percentage calculations (pct * bar_width / 100)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Smooth visual representation of progress across different phase types

patterns_discovered.name: ANSI code embedding with RESET sequences
patterns_discovered.description: All color codes wrap specific elements with `format!("{color}element{RESET}")` to prevent color bleed
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents unintended color spillover to adjacent content

patterns_discovered.name: Test assertion helpers
patterns_discovered.description: assert_has_ansi() and assert_has_box_chars() validate presence of visual elements independent of layout
patterns_discovered.benefit: Robust tests that don't break on minor formatting changes

gotchas_discovered.description: Narrow terminals (<40 cols) require saturation arithmetic to prevent negative fill calculations; saturating_sub() used throughout
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Saturate all width calculations; verify tests with width=40, 80, 120

gotchas_discovered.issue: ANSI escape sequence visibility in tests
gotchas_discovered.description: Tests must check for raw \x1b character codes, not rendered strings; contains() on byte sequences is required
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Use assert!(s.contains('\x1b')) and exact hex codes like "\x1b[93m" for state colors

gotchas_discovered.description: Exit codes only shown when state == 'failed'; must check state before appending to avoid spurious output on done/merged cards
Cards with 0% progress should not display '%' (indicates no progress tracking); must check progress > 0
Accounting for ┌ and ┐ (1 column each) in header requires subtracting 2 from width before calculating fill
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: let inner = w.saturating_sub(2); then subtract title length

approach_details.strategy: Implement trait methods in order of dependency: section header (container), card row (content), progress bar (detail), footer (summary). Provide comprehensive unit test coverage for ANSI codes, Unicode characters, and width adaptation.
execution: ✓ Defined state_color_16() with 5 state colors + default white (bright 90-97 range)
✓ render_section_header(): BOLD color + uppercase label + width-adaptive fill + box chars ┌─┐
✓ render_card_row(): accent stripe + marker + title + optional provider/elapsed/progress/exit_code
✓ render_progress(): 16-cell bar (████░░) + percentage + optional phase glyph
✓ render_footer(): stats summary with │ separators + width-adaptive fill + box chars └─┘
✓ Added 60+ unit tests with assertions for ANSI presence, box characters, content inclusion, and width adaptation

recommendations.suggestion: Extract ANSI color codes to a static constants module (e.g., colors.rs) to centralize 16-color palette and reduce duplication across renderer levels
Add integration tests rendering multiple cards (5-20 across all states) with realistic widths (40, 80, 120, 160) to verify layout does not wrap or truncate unexpectedly
Document the ANSI 16-color mapping (bright blue=pending, bright yellow=running, etc.) in code comments or RENDERING.md for future maintainers
For large card counts (100+), consider caching state_color_16() results or batching string allocations with a mutable String builder instead of Vec<String>.join()
Consider adding optional ASCII-only fallback mode (e.g., [▶] instead of ▶, #### instead of ████) for terminals that don't support Unicode
